name: Synthetic Data Factory
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Synthetic data generation — privacy-preserving record synthesis, schema-aware tabular generation, natural-language text synthesis, statistical distribution matching, and edge-case amplification for underrepresented classes

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for large-scale batch generation runs

services.name: Azure Machine Learning
services.purpose: Statistical validation pipeline — distribution comparison (KS test, chi-squared), privacy metrics (k-anonymity, differential privacy ε), utility scoring, and bias detection across generated datasets

dev.note: Free tier, small compute for validation scripts

prod.note: Managed compute for validation pipelines, ~50 runs/day

enterprise.note: GPU compute for GAN/VAE-based generation, ~200 runs/day

services.name: Azure Blob Storage
services.purpose: Dataset storage — source schema snapshots, generated synthetic datasets (Parquet/CSV/JSONL), validation reports, lineage metadata, and versioned dataset catalogs

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Orchestration layer — generation job dispatch, schema parsing, privacy constraint enforcement, batch chunking, validation trigger, and dataset publishing workflow

enterprise.note: High-memory for large schema processing, ~20M executions/month

services.name: Azure Data Factory
services.purpose: Pipeline orchestration — source data profiling, schema extraction, generation job scheduling, validation pipeline chaining, and output dataset delivery to downstream consumers

enterprise.note: ~10K pipeline runs/month, cross-region, change data capture

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Source database credentials, Azure OpenAI keys, ML workspace secrets, storage access keys, and encryption keys for sensitive schema metadata

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Generation pipeline observability — records generated per run, privacy score distribution, validation pass rates, generation latency, token consumption tracking

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for simple tabular row generation (names, addresses, IDs) and reserve GPT-4o for complex correlated record synthesis (medical histories, financial transactions) — reduces AI costs by 50-70%
Generate synthetic data in large batches during off-peak hours — Azure OpenAI has lower contention at night, and batch jobs can use lower-priority compute in Azure ML
Cache schema analysis results — source schemas rarely change, so a 24-hour cache avoids redundant profiling runs that consume ML compute
Use differential privacy noise injection post-generation instead of per-record privacy checks — validates privacy guarantees at the dataset level in a single pass rather than per-row evaluation
Store generated datasets as Parquet instead of CSV — 60-70% smaller storage footprint and faster downstream consumption for ML training pipelines
Implement generation deduplication — detect near-duplicate synthetic records and regenerate only unique variants, reducing redundant OpenAI token consumption
